job_description.summary: Build backend services and troubleshoot production issues.
responsibilities: Debug incidents and communicate root cause

question: Describe a specific time you debugged a backend API issue. What was the incident, how did you diagnose it, what did you change, and what was the outcome?
expected_good_answer_points: Describes one concrete production or project incident
candidate_answer: In one project, an endpoint was timing out. I checked logs and saw repeated expensive queries. I added an index and changed the query shape, then tested the endpoint with sample load. Response time dropped from around 2 seconds to under 300 milliseconds.

criteria.name: Technical Accuracy
criteria.description: Correctness, relevance, and depth of technical explanation

criteria.name: Specific Evidence
criteria.description: Uses concrete examples, implementation details, decisions, metrics, or outcomes

criteria.name: Problem-Solving Process
criteria.description: Shows clear reasoning, decomposition, debugging, or tradeoff analysis

criteria.name: Communication Clarity
criteria.description: Answer is structured, understandable, and concise

criteria.name: Role Relevance
criteria.description: Answer maps clearly to the job requirements and target competency

rating_anchors.1: Very weak: no relevant evidence, incorrect, vague, or unrelated answer
rating_anchors.2: Weak: partially relevant but shallow, incomplete, or contains major gaps
rating_anchors.3: Acceptable: mostly relevant answer with some concrete evidence but limited depth
rating_anchors.4: Strong: clear, relevant, evidence-based answer with good reasoning and details
rating_anchors.5: Excellent: deep, specific, well-structured answer with tradeoffs, outcomes, and strong job alignment
